fact: Every StatefulWidget that creates a TextEditingController, AnimationController, FocusNode, or StreamSubscription MUST dispose it in dispose(). Forgetting causes memory leaks.
Always check `if (!mounted) return;` before using BuildContext after any `await`. Widget may be unmounted during the async gap, causing 'setState() called after dispose' crashes.

Use `ref.watch()` only inside Widget.build(). Use `ref.read()` in event callbacks, onPressed, initState, and async methods. Using ref.watch in callbacks causes 'Cannot use ref.watch outside widget lifecycle' errors.
StateNotifier state MUST be updated with `state = state.copyWith(...)`. Never mutate state properties directly. Direct mutation breaks Riverpod's change detection.
Every list screen MUST paginate using `.range(offset, offset + pageSize - 1)` on Supabase queries. No pagination will crash the app with large datasets (school with 2000 students).
Use `autoDispose` for providers tied to a specific screen lifecycle. Without autoDispose, providers outlive their screen and hold stale data.
Use `const` constructors everywhere possible. `const Text('Hello')`, `const EdgeInsets.all(16)`, `const Icon(Icons.home)`. Dramatically reduces widget rebuilds.

Real-time Supabase channels (supabase.from('x').stream(...).listen(...)) MUST be cancelled in dispose() via subscription.cancel(). Forgetting causes channels to accumulate and fire events on disposed widgets.

When adding a new @freezed model, adding @riverpod annotation, or adding @JsonSerializable: run `flutter pub run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs` to regenerate .freezed.dart and .g.dart files. Commit BOTH the source and generated files.

The standard Flutter validation pipeline before any commit: (1) dart fix --apply, (2) flutter analyze, (3) flutter test, (4) flutter test --coverage. If any step fails, stop and fix before proceeding.

N+1 query pattern in providers: never loop over a list and make individual Supabase calls for each item. Fetch related data in a single join or use .in_() for batch fetches. Known issue: student list loads parents separately.

Use `FutureProvider.autoDispose.family<ReturnType, ParamType>` for parameterized data fetching in screens. Example: `studentProvider(studentId)` — reacts to navigation params and disposes when screen exits.
Always use named routes (GoRouter) for main navigation. Direct `Navigator.push` bypasses role guards. All new routes must be added to `lib/core/router/app_router.dart`.

Multi-step database operations (create invoice + update payment_status + debit wallet) MUST use Supabase RPCs (server-side functions) for atomicity. Client-side multi-step ops have no transaction guarantees and can leave data inconsistent.
